Class CombinationQueue

java.lang.Object
com.github.mrgarbagegamer.CombinationQueue
public class CombinationQueue extends Object
A thin, type-safe wrapper around a JCTools MpmcArrayQueue designed exclusively for transferring WorkBatch objects between producer and consumer threads.

Architectural Role

This class serves as a high-performance communication channel between CombinationGeneratorTask producer threads and TestClickCombination consumer threads ("monkeys"). Its sole purpose is to enqueue and dequeue WorkBatch instances, which is the cornerstone of the solver's low-contention batching architecture. By batching thousands of combinations into a single queue operation, it dramatically reduces synchronization overhead.

Implementation Details

It is implemented as a thin wrapper over MpmcArrayQueue to provide a clear, domain-specific API. The queue is bounded and operates in a lock-free manner, using relaxed (non-blocking) offers and polls for maximum throughput.

Thread Safety

This class is thread-safe for multiple producers and multiple consumers, as guaranteed by the underlying JCTools queue. However, the WorkBatch objects passed through it are not thread-safe. A safe hand-off protocol is required, where producers do not modify a batch after it has been offered to the queue.

Since:
2025.04 - Multi-threaded Architecture Introduction
See Also:
  • CombinationQueueArray
Performance Characteristics:
O(1) amortized for enqueue/dequeue operations and field accesses.
Threading Model:
Thread-safe via JCTools "magic".
Algorithm Details:
Lock-free, bounded MPMC queue with relaxed semantics using JCTools wizardry.
Memory Management:
Fixed memory overhead for the queue structure.

  • Field Details

    • QUEUE_SIZE

      private final int QUEUE_SIZE
      The fixed capacity of the queue.

      This size represents a trade-off:

      • Larger Queue: Reduces the chance of a generator failing to enqueue a new batch, but increases the solver's overall memory footprint.
      • Smaller Queue: Minimizes memory overhead but increases the risk of monkeys becoming starved for work if generators are temporarily blocked.
      A small size is chosen because the queue holds large WorkBatch objects, making memory a primary consideration.

      Note that, per MpmcArrayQueue(int), the capacity will be rounded up to the next power of two if it is not already.

      Since:
      2025.07 - Enqueuing WorkBatch Objects
      See Also:
      • ConcurrentSequencedCircularArrayQueue.capacity()
      • Constant Field Values
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access time.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe; immutable field.
      Memory Management:
      Minimal footprint of 4 bytes as an int.

    • queue

      private final MpmcArrayQueue<WorkBatch> queue
      The underlying JCTools queue that holds WorkBatch objects.
      Since:
      2025.07 - Enqueuing WorkBatch Objects
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) amortized for enqueue/dequeue operations, O(1) capacity access, and O(QUEUE_SIZE) size access.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe via JCTools "magic".
      Memory Management:
      Fixed memory overhead for the queue structure.

  • Method Details

    • getCapacity

      public int getCapacity()
      Returns the fixed capacity of the queue.
      Returns:
      the queue's capacity.
      Since:
      2025.07 - Enqueuing WorkBatch Objects
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) field access.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe; returns an immutable field.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

    • add

      public boolean add(WorkBatch workBatch)
      Offers a WorkBatch to the queue using a non-blocking, relaxed operation.

      This method may occasionally fail (return false) even if the queue is not full, which is an acceptable trade-off for higher throughput.

      Parameters:
      workBatch - the batch to add.
      Returns:
      true if the batch was successfully added, false otherwise.
      Since:
      2025.07 - Enqueuing WorkBatch Objects
      See Also:
      • MpmcArrayQueue.relaxedOffer(Object)
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) amortized enqueue operation.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe via JCTools "magic".
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate; uses pre-allocated queue slots.

    • getWorkBatch

      public WorkBatch getWorkBatch()
      Retrieves a WorkBatch from the queue using a non-blocking, relaxed operation.

      This method may occasionally fail (return null) even if the queue is not empty, which is an acceptable trade-off for higher throughput.

      Returns:
      a WorkBatch if one was available, or null otherwise.
      Since:
      2025.07 - Enqueuing WorkBatch Objects
      See Also:
      • MpmcArrayQueue.relaxedPoll()
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) amortized dequeue operation.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe via JCTools "magic".
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate; returns references to existing objects.

    • isEmpty

      public boolean isEmpty()
      Checks if the queue is empty.

      Note: In a concurrent environment, the result of this call is only a snapshot in time and may be immediately outdated.

      Returns:
      true if the queue was empty at the time of the call, false otherwise.
      Since:
      2025.07 - Enqueuing WorkBatch Objects
      See Also:
      • ConcurrentSequencedCircularArrayQueue.isEmpty()
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) call to queue method.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe via JCTools "magic". May be stale immediately after return.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

Performance-optimized brute-force solver for hexagonal grid Lights Out puzzles
